title: Random "authentication required" on podman 4.6.1
body:
### Issue Description

Hi,

After an upgrade on our redhat 8, our podman version has been upgraded from podman `4.4.1` to `4.6.1`. Things have worked flawlessly for 2 years so far.

Since this sanity patching, we have observed random "authorization required", without any root cause. It "seems" that it happens at the very end of the push process (internal registry, based on https://hub.docker.com/_/registry), but setting podman to debug log level does not give more information, except that it seems to fail when trying to write the manifest at the very end.

Our credentials are in `~/.docker/config.json`, but moving them to to`${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json` does not fix this randomness.

After this fail, relaunching a simple `podman push XXX` works 2 times on 3.

We have inspected LDAP logs for auth. failures, but no bind is processed. Looks like it's at client side that things go wrong.

We have `yum downgraded` this morning to `4.4.1`, things are back to normal. After reading the changelog, we can't see any deprecation that could give us a clue.

Please let me know if you need more information.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Redhat 8, install latest patches
2. podman --version should be 4.6.1
3. build and push some images, you may observe some authentication required failures


### Describe the results you received

```
+ podman push docker-registry.XXXXXX.com/whatever/whatever:24.2.X
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:d66e821df3f97b437d52fd6557fb1ef3ce68709a542d279ca8227fd333ca051e

Copying blob sha256:96b8c4e38de56d91a792604e14069e9e7f98a3649fd82caf8a34db63f2cd8aeb
Copying blob sha256:79a803ab33e2500155e0192a5ee33f99b5bc43ebe368b67a179a7344387509d4
Copying blob sha256:c5637af9c584264e892755c3ed8983092d0868fac79bdf900f57ca3d0ef0b99e
Copying blob sha256:f9633dda4d3f05e30d228fadd92af0b94a4abf9040cfeae997827541b53914a9
Copying blob sha256:cea0d813679af597ebda4da839e5de58c35e5496c9e1b6fadfd6b8928cf61959
Copying blob sha256:2bd11f6e753285481287ea0a570084640d6fed6bc841f56412fee2d3e9701ba7
Copying blob sha256:cee3743a313ec4040f67ae1b0613a6ae73952acb2903fd19ba5e0020bf1c4d16
Error: writing blob: uploading layer chunked: authentication required
```

### Describe the results you expected

pushed withour any error

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
If you have an issue with a RHEL version you should contact RHEL support unless you know the bug still exists in the latest version.
As upstream devs we will always ask you to first test with the latest upstream version, especially if the reproducer is "random" and cannot quickly be verified by us.

I can fully understand that an issue on an 'outdated' version, on a paid OS, is not something you may look at. But was wondering if any clue on your side could lead us to any workaround, or at least troobleshoot what may happen on v4.6.1 (vs 4.4.1). We are unfortunately stuck on RH8 for the moment, and as "non root" users on those VM, we are dependent of what is available in the RH repositories.

Feel free to close this issue if you think this is something at RedHat side, and we'll open a ticket at RH support. Thanks again.

It is not so much that I think the issue is with RHEL, RHEL has its own bug process that should be followed for best experience. In the end it is likely that the issue is still in our code however as far as upstream is concerned we fix the bug then release a new version eventually and until RHEL picks that up you may wait another 6+ months depending on the timing.
So it is in your best interest to go through the Red Hat channels because you then can request to backport the fixes to the version you are actually using right now.

I'd also echo what @Luap99 said.  There is also the valuable enterprise support to help resolve such issues - even if they are not caused by Podman.

While reaching out to RH support, you may already start taking a look at the registry you are using if possible.  I have no recollection of this symptom/issue.  In case you are using a proxy, try running without it if possible.  Proxies are a common source of transient failures.
Thanks, we'll go this way. Closing now.
